panels.title: Search p95 Latency
panels.description: Developer quality loop guardrail: large quality improvements are not useful if every search becomes too slow for agent use.

panels.title: Domain Diversity p50
panels.description: Median unique domains in returned search results. Low diversity can indicate provider collapse or duplicate-heavy ranking.

panels.title: Semantic Cache Hit Score p50
panels.description: Median similarity for accepted semantic cache hits. Watch this when tuning semantic-cache thresholds and TTLs.

panels.title: Diversity Removals / sec
panels.description: How often reranking removes near-duplicate domains/results. Spikes can reveal noisy provider overlap or overly broad rewrites.

panels.title: Provider Result Yield
panels.description: Average returned results per successful provider call. Useful for spotting providers that are fast but not useful.

targets.expr: sum by (provider_name) (rate(web_search_provider_results_total{service_name="$service", deployment_environment=~"$environment"}[5m])) / clamp_min(sum by (provider_name) (rate(web_search_provider_calls_total{service_name="$service", deployment_environment=~"$environment", provider_status="success"}[5m])), 0.001)

panels.title: Query Length Distribution
panels.description: p50/p95 query length entering the search pipeline. Long agent queries often correlate with worse keyword-search quality unless rewrite works well.

panels.title: Domain Diversity Distribution
panels.description: p50/p95 unique domains in returned results. Use with provider yield to diagnose duplicate-heavy or single-domain-heavy searches.

panels.title: Rerank Score Distribution
panels.description: Distribution of reranker scores by result position. Low or flat score bands suggest the candidate pool is weak or the reranker is not discriminating.

panels.title: RRF Score Distribution
panels.description: Merged candidate score distribution before final rerank. Helps identify whether RRF is producing a meaningful candidate ordering.

panels.title: Rewrite Policy Mix
panels.description: Quality lens for rewrite routing. Sudden fallback/bypass shifts usually explain provider coverage and relevance changes.

panels.title: Provider Contribution Matrix
panels.description: Last 6h provider volume, output, and health. This is the first stop when a provider is expensive/noisy or stops contributing useful candidates.

panels.title: Semantic Cache TTL and Hybrid Search
panels.description: Accepted cache TTLs and hybrid-search outcomes. Use this to tune freshness rules and catch stale semantic hits.

panels.title: Candidate Survival by Stage
panels.description: Raw stage-by-stage survival for candidate URLs. Use this to see where results disappear between provider search, merge, rerank, and final answer windows.

targets.rawSql: WITH stage_counts AS ( SELECT stage, count(*) AS candidates, count(DISTINCT run_key) AS runs FROM kindly_analytics.vw_candidate_survival WHERE $__timeFilter(recorded_at) GROUP BY stage ), provider_base AS ( SELECT candidates AS provider_candidates FROM stage_counts WHERE stage = 'provider' ) SELECT stage, candidates, runs, round(100.0 * candidates / nullif((SELECT provider_candidates FROM provider_base), 0), 2) AS provider_retention_pct FROM stage_counts ORDER BY CASE stage WHEN 'provider' THEN 1 WHEN 'branch' THEN 2 WHEN 'merged' THEN 3 WHEN 'reranked' THEN 4 WHEN 'final' THEN 5 ELSE 99 END

panels.title: Provider Yield Over Time
panels.description: Average number of raw results returned by each provider call, based on persisted provider search events. This exposes providers that are low-yield even when they succeed.

targets.rawSql: SELECT date_trunc('minute', recorded_at) AS time, provider_name AS metric, avg(result_count) AS value FROM ( SELECT recorded_at, json_extract_string(payload_json, '$.provider_name') AS provider_name, CAST(json_extract_string(payload_json, '$.result_count') AS DOUBLE) AS result_count FROM kindly_analytics.analytics_event_raw WHERE event_name = 'provider.search.result' AND $__timeFilter(recorded_at) ) GROUP BY 1, 2 ORDER BY 1

panels.title: SearXNG Engine Quality
panels.description: Engine-level contribution and score quality for SearXNG results. This is the panel to inspect when SearXNG turns noisy or one engine dominates the candidate pool.

targets.rawSql: WITH searxng_engine_rows AS ( SELECT recorded_at, run_key, provider_name, json_extract_string(engine.value, '$') AS engine_name, raw_score, score, category, published_date FROM kindly_analytics.vw_provider_results CROSS JOIN json_each(source_engines_json) AS engine WHERE $__timeFilter(recorded_at) AND provider_name = 'searxng' ) SELECT engine_name, count(*) AS results, count(DISTINCT run_key) AS runs, round(avg(raw_score), 4) AS avg_raw_score, round(avg(score), 4) AS avg_merged_score, count(*) FILTER (WHERE category IS NOT NULL) AS categorized_results FROM searxng_engine_rows GROUP BY engine_name ORDER BY results DESC, engine_name
